channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: Officials are investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead wait anxiously for answers.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bodycam video: Sisters arrested for attacking gate agent at airport
item.description: Newly-released body camera video shows the moment two sisters were arrested for allegedly assaulting a Frontier Airlines gate agent.
item.dc:creator: Sara Kitchin, NewsNation

item.title: Erika Kirk addresses grief in heartfelt social media post one month after husband's assassination
item.description: Charlie Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, reflected on the grieving process in a social media post, describing the non-linear nature of loss after his Sept. 10 assassination at Utah Valley University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Halloween auction for 'The Conjuring' house canceled
item.description: The Halloween auction for the Rhode Island farmhouse made famous by the blockbuster "The Conjuring" has been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Guernelli, Fox 5

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WFLA, NBC

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ground stop issued at busy Atlanta airport following fire incident with 'strong gas fumes,' FAA confirms
item.description: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport experienced a ground stop Friday afternoon due to a fire incident involving "gas fumes," according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TGI Fridays offering free food for some federal workers amid shutdown
item.description: The promotion will be available all day, every day during the two-week period, unless the shutdown ends before Oct. 23.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 22 states are in a recession or on the brink: Moody’s
item.description: It’s more sobering news for the economy as last week U.S. hiring plans fell to their lowest level since 2009.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Connecticut man, woman charged with 31 counts of animal cruelty after dead animals found in trailer, bus
item.description: "That's crazy, man," Julius Bogues, who lives in Waterbury, said. "We ride by here and you never know what's going on right around you every day, very bizarre.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, NewsNation

item.title: Man convicted of killing a 15-year-old girl in her home in 2001 is executed by injection in Indiana
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was executed by injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year. Roy Lee Ward, 53, was put to death at the Indiana State Prison in Michigan City.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Green group files Hatch Act complaints against HHS, Forest Service over 'partisan' shutdown messaging
item.description: An environmental advocacy group has filed complaints under the Hatch Act against the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and the Forest Service over what it described as “partisan” messaging blaming Democrats for the ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Palestinians pack up and move north toward their homes in Gaza after ceasefire goes into effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians displaced by the war in Gaza started walking north Friday toward homes they were forced to flee — or what remains of them — after the Israeli military announced a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas had gone into effect.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he looking at 'massive increase' in tariffs against China
item.description: President Donald Trump says “there seems to be no reason” to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping as part of an upcoming trip to South Korea after China restricted exports of rare earths needed for American industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia: Attorneys argue smuggling charges should be thrown out for 'vindictive' prosecution
item.description: Attorneys for Kilmar Abrego Garcia were in court Friday morning arguing the human smuggling charges against their client should be dismissed for vindictive prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mayer, NewsNation

item.title: Fear and vigilance rise as attacks on houses of worship intensify worldwide
item.description: Every week hundreds of millions of people around the world gather to worship in peace. But for some, there comes a day when deadly violence invades their sacred spaces and shatters that sense of sanctuary and safety. It happened recently at a synagogue in England and two churches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, PETER SMITH and TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Timeline of the Palisades Fire manhunt: From alleged midnight spark to Florida arrest
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht, 29, was arrested on federal arson charges for allegedly starting the destructive Palisades Fire that began on New Year's Day in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lyft scraps feature that let drivers see how often riders tip
item.description: Lyft has scrapped a test feature that showed some drivers how often passengers tip before accepting a ride.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Trump does not win Nobel Peace Prize despite Israel-Hamas deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frustrations turn to heated confrontations as shutdown drags on
item.description: Partisan standoffs in the Capitol are bubbling into tense public confrontations as the government shutdown heads into its 10th day — with no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: Tropical weather in the Atlantic and Pacific oceans is causing dangerous conditions in the United States, Mexico and the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers advance to NLCS, beat Phillies 2-1 on Kerkering's 11th-inning error
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Philadelphia Phillies and Los Angeles Dodgers didn't blink as the pressure built, inning after scoreless inning. Both teams pushed a single run across, forcing extra innings. Then came one of postseason baseball's craziest endings.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A history of President Trump's legal battles with New York Attorney General Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The day she was elected New York's attorney general, Letitia James blasted President Donald Trump as a “con man” and “carnival barker" and pledged to scrutinize his public policies and personal business dealings.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas Jewish community expresses hope, hesitation on peace deal between Israel, Hamas
item.description: Israel and Hamas are taking a major step toward ending their two-year-long war. The new deal calls for Hamas to release all hostages and for Israel to pull back its forces from Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After a police pursuit of a huge inflatable pumpkin, body cam video shows officer struggling with it
item.description: PARMA HEIGHTS, Ohio (AP) — The subject of an Ohio police pursuit took off down the road, a flash of orange in the darkness, but officers eventually caught up and grabbed the escapee: a runaway inflatable pumpkin as tall as them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico's president says Trump open to individual trade deals instead of USMCA
item.description: "There might be revisions that create bilateral deals instead of involving the three countries because some things are more important between Mexico and the United Sates or between Canada and the United States," said Sheinbaum. "Not everything has to be trilateral.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Texas National Guard troops now protecting federal property in Chicago area, official says
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A small number of National Guard troops has started protecting federal property in the Chicago area and assisting law enforcement in Memphis on Wednesday, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: These US cities spend the most – and least – on groceries, study finds
item.description: As grocery prices continue to march higher, some U.S. cities are feeling the burden more than others, a new study found.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4
